summary: Russian operators from the "Rubicon" Center intercepted over 2,000 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs using FPV drones, accounting for more than 80 enemy UAV models, with over 75% of intercepts targeting 12 specific fixed-wing types. On April 11 and 13, 2026, Rubicon units destroyed 110 and 191 targets respectively in the Donbas, Belgorod, and Krasny Liman directions, including Ukrainian UAVs and personnel, while coordinating with Russian Aerospace Forces to strike UAV launch sites. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to kill Russian soldiers attempting to assault positions on motorcycles and to eliminate a Russian soldier who feigned surrender before fleeing. Ukrainian electronic warfare crews deployed jamming tactics, including verbal warnings giving FPV drones 15 seconds to land. A Ukrainian Marine Brigade unit survived a Russian FPV strike in the Kostiantynivka direction after detecting the drone with a handheld detector. Separately, Ukrainian firm XDOWN unveiled the STUD, a 2.7 kg, 300 km/h FPV drone with a 64 km range and 800 g payload, featuring a Switchblade-style foldable design.
